title: Bracteocarpus kinabaluensis
taxonomy: Bracteocarpus kinabaluensis is a plant species belonging to the family Amaranthaceae. It is a member of the genus Bracteocarpus, which is commonly found in Southeast Asia. The plant is classified under the kingdom Plantae, further divided into the phylum Magnoliophyta and the class Magnoliopsida. Bracteocarpus kinabaluensis is a relatively rare species, endemic to the mountainous regions of Borneo. As a member of the Amaranthaceae family, it shares characteristics with other plants in this group, such as its succulent leaves and annual growth habit.

intro: Bracteocarpus kinabaluensis, a rare and endemic plant species native to the mountainous regions of Sabah, Malaysia, is a type of medicinal herb belonging to the family Malpighiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including inflammation and fever, due to its reported health benefits in reducing oxidative stress and improving immune function. Bioactive constituents of Bracteocarpus kinabaluensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared into a decoction or a tincture, which is commonly used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions.
